relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The whole assembly gathers at Shiloh and the tent of meeting is set in place.
Seven tribes still wait for their inheritance to be divided.
Joshua challenges delay in taking up the unfinished allocation.
Three witnesses from each tribe are sent to walk and write the land.
The remaining territory is to be described in seven portions between Judah and Joseph.
Written portions are brought back so lots can be cast before YHWH.
Levi, Gad, Reuben, and half Manasseh are already accounted for differently.
The surveyors rise with Joshua's command to walk, write, return, and submit the land to lot.
The land is turned into a scroll of cities and portions.
At Shiloh, Joshua divides the remaining land before YHWH.
Benjamin's lot rises between Judah and Joseph.
The northern border climbs from Jordan past Jericho toward Beth-aven.
Luz, Bethel, and Ataroth-addar shape the western descent.
The boundary turns toward Kiriath-jearim on the Judah edge.
The southern line begins again from Kiriath-jearim toward Nephtoah.
Hinnom, Rephaim, Jebusite Jerusalem, and En-rogel mark a charged landscape.
En-shemesh, Geliloth, Adummim, and Bohan's stone continue the line.
The border descends toward the Arabah.
Beth-hoglah, the Salt Sea, and Jordan close the southern edge.
Jordan seals Benjamin's eastern side.
Jericho, Beth-hoglah, and Emek-keziz begin the city list.
Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, and Bethel follow.
Avvim, Parah, and Ophrah are named.
Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba close twelve cities.
Gibeon, Ramah, and Beeroth begin the next group.
Mizpeh, Chephirah, and Mozah follow.
Rekem, Irpeel, and Taralah are listed.
Zela, Haeleph, Jebusite Jerusalem, Gibeah, and Kiriath complete Benjamin's inheritance.

contemplative_note: Joshua 18 turns territory into writing: witnesses walk the land, cities become portions, and lots are cast at Shiloh. Ethical memory must remember that a scroll can organize claims, but it cannot erase the living people already inside the named places.
